title: How to Know When You've Hit a Money Block (vs a Money Fact)
subtitle: A reading on the difference between a psychological stuck point and an external financial constraint
excerpt: A short reading on how to tell the difference between a money block and a money fact. Covers five markers that indicate a block rather than a fact, and explains why both need attention but in different ways.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education as part of the beginner entry points for money work.

practice.whenToUse: Read when you're not sure whether what's stopping you is an internal money block or an external financial reality, and you want a simple way to tell the difference.

content.text: A money fact is real. A money block is also real. The thing that fixes each of them is different. Applying inner work to a practical problem doesn't help much. Applying practical action to a block doesn't help much either. The first step is knowing which one you're dealing with.
What a money fact looks like

A money fact is an external constraint: not enough income to cover costs, a debt at a specific interest rate, a bill on a specific date. Facts respond to information, planning, and direct action. When you address the fact, the feeling connected to it generally eases.
What a money block looks like

A money block is an internal pattern: a belief, a habitual feeling, or a story about who you are in relation to money. Blocks don't respond to information alone. You can know exactly what to do next and still not do it. You can increase your income and watch spending rise to match it.
Five markers that suggest you're dealing with a block rather than a fact:
The anxiety persists even when the numbers are fine.
You know what to do next and you don't do it.
The same pattern keeps appearing in different forms.
You feel disproportionately anxious about amounts that are objectively manageable.
When income goes up, expenses rise to match it and you end up in the same position.
Both can be true at once

Most people have a real financial situation and a block pattern running at the same time. The financial situation needs practical attention. The block needs inner work. Doing only one leaves the problem half-addressed. The work in this library covers the inner layer. The practical layer is yours to handle alongside it.
Where to start if you're not sure
If you can't tell which is running, start with the journal prompt for the money question you've been avoiding, or the first tapping script. Both are designed to locate the thing before working on it.
Written for homemade.education as part of the beginner entry points for money work.
